route_inventory.attribution: project database; KPI id/project originate from the selected project connection
project database; outcome and KPI-link joins stay on the selected project connection
task -> outcome -> claim/outcome spend -> KPI links; spend rows deduplicated by id
KPI -> outcome links -> outcome task ids -> task/outcome/claim spend
project task inventory -> per-task tally -> KPI totals; verified outcomes are the denominator
deliverable links retain task project+id, milestone, proof bucket, KPI project namespace, and proven/in-review split

writer_inventory.transaction: one project connection resolves optional outcome, applies request-id idempotency, inserts spend and tally.usage_reported activity
one project connection inserts outcome and tally.outcome_recorded activity
one project connection reads outcome, merges evidence, updates verification, and inserts activity
one project connection reads outcome, records rejection evidence/status, and inserts activity
one project connection inserts KPI and tally.kpi_created activity
one project connection updates KPI value and inserts evidence-bearing activity
one project connection resolves outcome+KPI, inserts the link, and inserts tally.outcome_kpi_linked activity

writer_policy.reader_rule: the proposed service may expose committed read models only; all seven writers remain on the monolith
writer_policy.unsafe_attribution_rule: a read process is not authorized while writers can persist conflicting task, claim, outcome, or KPI attribution

project_scope.read_binding: every read requires Query(...) and resolve_project(project)
project_scope.write_binding: every write resolves the body project before principal authorization
project_scope.storage_isolation: one SQLite database per Switchboard project

blocking_findings.detail: When both task_id and outcome_id are supplied, the outcome task is not compared with task_id; claim ownership is not validated.
task_id and claim_id are accepted without proving that they exist and belong to the same project/task before the outcome is inserted.

preserved_read_contracts: task tallies deduplicate spend reachable by task, outcome, or claim
KPI tallies include only verified contribution in the denominator
deliverable tallies retain task project and proof bucket and namespace KPIs by project

sqlite_contention.model: one monolith attribution-bundle writer plus three standalone read workers

resource_budget.measurement_scope: live post-Deliverables host with monolith, Auth, Tasks, Coord, and Deliverables active
resource_budget.projection_basis: 128 MiB hard cap consistent with the prior read-service independence budget; swap is excluded from resident reserve

G1_route_repository_writer_inventory.reason: All thirteen Tally routes are classified as six day-one reads and seven monolith-owned writers with concrete repository and transaction boundaries.

G2_attribution_integrity.reason: Spend/outcome writers do not fail closed on conflicting task/outcome/claim parents, so existing read joins cannot yet be treated as an invariant-safe standalone ledger.

G3_project_scope.reason: Every route resolves an explicit project and storage remains isolated by project database.

G4_writer_transaction_boundaries.reason: All seven writers and their audit effects have named monolith transaction boundaries and remain outside day-one service scope.

G5_sqlite_contention.reason: Executable WAL probe requires zero lock errors, zero attribution snapshot mismatches, and quick_check=ok.

G6_resource_budget.reason: A 128 MiB Tally process would leave 470921216 bytes available, 65949696 bytes below the required 512 MiB resident reserve.

nogo_exit.next_action: Keep Tally in-process. Re-evaluate only after attribution parents fail closed and at least 65949696 additional bytes of resident headroom are demonstrated (plus safety margin).
